market.advice: Trend is healthy underneath: SPY above its 50-day, ~70% of names above their MA50, VIX calm at 16.4. But futures are pointing lower pre-open on fresh US-Iran escalation and Strait of Hormuz risk lifting oil. Respect the uptrend, but let the open settle before chasing — favor clean bases over extended names, keep stops honest, and note energy is the one group the geopolitics is helping.

picks.thesis: Northern Trust broke to a new 52-week high out of a tight VCP base with relative strength at the highs — a clean, un-extended leadership move in financials. TradingView agrees (BUY).
strengths: New high on a tight VCP base
risks: Q2 earnings in ~9 days — size accordingly
RSI 72 is warm but not extended

picks.thesis: Crocs cleared to a new high with RSI still only mid-range (59) — plenty of room before overbought. TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY, so quant, technicals and a second opinion all line up.
risks: HEYDUDE brand still the soft spot
Consumer-discretionary sensitivity if the tape turns risk-off

picks.thesis: Arista broke out with RSI 59 — the cleanest large-cap AI-networking setup on the board, not chasing. The AI data-center buildout is a direct revenue tailwind. TV: BUY.
Monster is right at a 52-week high out of a tight base — a defensive-consumer leader working while the tape is jittery. TV: BUY.
strengths: Low-beta ballast for a risk-off open

picks.thesis: Grainger broke out with RSI a cool 52 — an industrial-distribution compounder making new highs without being extended. TV: BUY.
risks: High absolute share price — position-size carefully

picks.thesis: Liquidia is the momentum outlier — +102% 3-mo — riding the YUTREPIA approval and PAH commercialization, and it broke out with RSI still only 62. TV: BUY. Higher-risk small-cap biotech; keep it a smaller line.
MetLife is at a new high with RS at the highs — an insurance leader benefiting from higher-for-longer yields on its investment book. TV: BUY.
Black Hills is a utility breakout that TradingView independently rates STRONG BUY — a defensive, income-y name catching a bid as the tape gets nervous. Both our scan and TV align.
risks: Rate-sensitive if yields spike on the oil move

picks.thesis: Eli Lilly is basing tightly just 3.8% off its high with RSI 65 — the mega-cap GLP-1 leader (Zepbound/Mounjaro) remains a secular grower. TV: BUY.
risks: Very high share price — size carefully

picks.thesis: F5 broke out on the strongest 3-mo momentum in large-cap networking (+48%) — application delivery/security demand is real. TV: BUY. RSI 74 is the one caution.
risks: RSI 73.9 — extended, prefer a pullback entry

picks.thesis: Terreno Realty made a new high and TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY — an industrial-REIT with pristine coastal logistics assets. Scan + TV agree.
risks: Rate-sensitive REIT if yields jump on oil

picks.thesis: Fortinet is basing 3.8% off its high with +105% 3-mo momentum and a strong FCF cyber model. TV: BUY. A held name that is also a top setup — conviction on both sides.
GE Aerospace broke out with RSI only 52 — aftermarket/services demand is a durable engine. Clean setup, but earnings are only ~3 days out, so treat this as an after-the-print name.
risks: Earnings in ~3 days — event risk is high
Wait for the print or keep it small

picks.thesis: Edwards Lifesciences is basing near highs (RSI 64) — structural-heart/TAVR leader with a clean chart. TV: BUY.
Corteva is the ag/materials diversifier here — a breakout TradingView independently rates STRONG BUY. Seeds & crop-protection with pricing power; scan + TV agree.
strengths: Sector diversification vs a tech-heavy list

picks.thesis: Nvidia pulled back ~10% from its high and RSI reset to 50 — an un-extended re-entry on the AI bellwether. TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY, so the reset looks like a base, not a breakdown. A held name.
risks: Highest-weight AI name — moves the whole tape

picks.thesis: Coca-Cola breakout that TradingView independently rates STRONG BUY — a defensive-consumer anchor for a nervous open. Scan + TV agree.
risks: Low growth — this is ballast, not a mover

picks.thesis: Airbnb broke out with RSI only 58 and TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY — travel/consumer momentum with room to run. Scan + TV agree.
Cisco broke out with RSI 53 and +48% 3-mo momentum — an AI-networking/security re-rating, and TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY. Un-extended and confirmed.
Targa Resources is the timely energy pick — a midstream breakout as the Iran/Hormuz escalation lifts oil. Permian NGL volumes are the growth engine. TV: BUY.
strengths: Energy tailwind on the geopolitical bid

picks.thesis: EOG is a second energy line with direct leverage to the oil move — a low-cost E&P basing near a breakout (RSI 57). The Hormuz risk premium is the near-term catalyst. TV: BUY.
strengths: Direct crude leverage into the oil spike
Low-cost premium acreage, strong balance sheet
risks: Oil is the whole thesis — reverses if the conflict de-escalates

picks.thesis: Apple is right at a new high (only 0.3% off) with RS at the highs — the most widely held name across the book, working cleanly. TV: BUY.
strengths: At new highs, RS at highs

picks.thesis: Travelers is a strong setup that TradingView rates STRONG BUY — but earnings are only ~4 days out and RSI is 77. Watch for the print, then act; don't chase into the event.
Innovative Industrial Properties is a top-scoring REIT breakout (TV STRONG BUY) but RSI 82 is severely overbought — wait for a pullback to the breakout level rather than chasing.
CSX broke to a new high but RSI 82.7 is overbought — a great rail setup that's gotten ahead of itself. Let it digest toward the breakout band before entering. Earnings ~10 days out.
Progyny has explosive +88% 3-mo momentum but RSI 89 is at a screaming extreme — pure momentum, not a low-risk entry. Watch for a controlled pullback; this is a high-volatility name.
Goldman reports earnings TOMORROW (d2e=1) — held across three accounts here. Don't add into the print; RSI 40 shows it's already soft. Let the report clear, then reassess.
Robinhood still sits 27% below its high but is rebuilding (+62% 3-mo, TV BUY). A held name that's basing, not breaking out — watch for a reclaim of the base high before adding.
risks: Still 27% below highs — not a breakout yet
High beta to retail risk appetite

picks.thesis: Snowflake is 7% off its high with +116% 3-mo momentum but RSI 72 is warm — a held AI-data name that's extended near-term. TV: BUY. Prefer a pullback to add.
